recommended_next_steps: Identify whether the top process is expected to hold this much memory.
Check recent deploys, traffic, cache growth, and data volume changes.
Correlate memory pressure with latency or OOM alerts.
unsafe_actions_avoided: Did not run disallowed first-response command: kill.
Did not run disallowed first-response command: systemctl restart.
Did not run disallowed first-response command: sysctl.
Safety policy observed: Do not kill processes without identifying ownership and blast radius.
Safety policy observed: Do not restart services before preserving memory and OOM evidence.
Safety policy observed: Do not change kernel memory settings during triage without explicit approval.

hypotheses_ranked.summary: Kernel OOM killer terminated a process

recommended_next_steps: Identify the killed process and owning service.
Compare the killed process memory growth to recent deploy or traffic changes.
Check whether memory limits, host sizing, or workload placement changed recently.
